attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway specialist who bridges decisive gaps on final climbs, disguising form before late-race accelerations. Shows ability to survive sustained climbs then accelerate when others fade.
team_tactics: Operates with freedom in breakaway situations rather than protected team leadership; positioned to exploit reduced group scenarios where tactical awareness trumps pure power.
terrain_preference: Medium-mountain stages with Cat-2 final climbs where climbing durability separates breakaway survivors; excels when terrain filters peloton but doesn't require pure climbing prowess.
vulnerability: Limited recent evidence of pure sprint finishes or high-mountain stages; effectiveness may depend on avoiding direct confrontation with pure climbers or sprinters in full-strength groups.
key_patterns: Wins exclusively from breakaways, never from bunch sprints or GC group finishes (2 wins)
Thrives in reduced groups on final climbs, using experience over power (2 observations)
Long gaps between WorldTour wins suggests opportunistic rather than consistent winning threat (3-year gap documented)
Demonstrates unexpected late-career climbing durability into late 30s (documented at age 37)
form_trajectory: positive: Returned to WorldTour winning form in 2022 at age 37 after 3-year drought, showing renewed climbing legs on sustained Cat-2 ascents
